channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump Approval Ratings Drop Amid Economic Concerns as Republicans Pursue Redistricting and Voting Measures Ahead of Midterms
item.description: The article discusses the declining approval ratings of US President Donald Trump, which has reached 62% disapproval according to a recent poll. Key issues include rising inflation and gas prices link...

item.title: Jonathan Calls Emir Sanusi ‘My Good Friend’ Despite Past Disagreements
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan sparked laughter during the public presentation of General Yakubu Gowon's autobiography when he referred to the 16th Emir of Kano, Alhaji Muhammadu Sanusi II, as 'My...

item.title: Heat and Storms to Affect Windsor-Essex Ahead of Midweek Cooldown
item.description: Residents of Windsor-Essex should prepare for continued hot weather and storm activity before a noticeable drop in temperatures is expected midweek. Meteorologists predict sweltering conditions will p...

item.title: Sindh Police Investigates High-Profile Narcotics Case Involving Pinky
item.description: KARACHI: Sindh Inspector General of Police, Javed Alam Odho, has stated that multiple prominent individuals may be identified during the ongoing investigations into the high-profile drug dealer, Anmol...

item.title: Thailand reduces visa-free stay duration for tourists from over 90 countries to address foreign crime concerns
item.description: Thailand has decided to shorten the visa-free stay period for travellers coming from more than 90 countries. The main reason is to control crime involving foreign nationals, as per government official...

item.title: High Carjacking Risk Persists for South African Drivers with 60 Daily Incidents
item.description: Carjackings in South Africa remain alarmingly high, with an average of 60 vehicles hijacked every day. Syndicates continue to target popular commuter vehicles like the Toyota Hilux, Volkswagen Polo, a...

item.title: Government Launches Major Transport Upgrade to Improve Dodoma Mobility
item.description: The Government of Tanzania, through the Ministry of Works and TANROADS, has officially signed the contract for the Dodoma Integrated and Sustainable Transport (DIST) Project on May 18, 2026. This proj...

item.title: Fourth Case of Meningitis B Confirmed in Reading Junior School Pupil Amid Local Outbreak
item.description: Health authorities in the UK have confirmed a fourth case of meningitis B in Reading. The latest patient is a pupil at Westwood Farm Junior School and is connected to the same social network as the pr...

item.title: Trump Allegedly Suggested U.S., China, and Russia Cooperate Against ICC During Beijing Summit
item.description: During the U.S.-China summit in Beijing, President Donald Trump reportedly proposed to Chinese President Xi Jinping that the United States, China, and Russia work together to oppose the International ...

item.title: Canadian Citizen Abroad Faces Repatriation Challenges Amid Terrorism Allegations
item.description: Wassim Boughadou, a 34-year-old Montreal-born man, is wanted by the RCMP on terrorism-related charges but is currently unable to return to Canada due to passport and repatriation issues. Arrested in T...

item.title: AFP Continues Enhancement of Structures in Philippine-held Features in West Philippine Sea
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) announced on Tuesday that it is proceeding with the construction, repair, and enhancement of various structures in the Philippine-held features within the Wes...

item.title: US and China: Understanding Economic and Demographic Shifts to Avoid Conflict
item.description: The article analyzes the current state of US-China relations, arguing that the long-feared Thucydides Trap — where a rising power challenges an established one — is no longer applicable. China, once a...

item.title: PNP Confirms Senator Bato Dela Rosa Left Senate in Sen. Robin Padilla's Toyota Fortuner
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) confirmed that Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa was seen leaving the Senate complex in a white Toyota Fortuner registered to Senator Robin Padilla shortly after a s...

item.title: A guide to the most soothing video game soundtracks
item.description: From the iconic, piano-led melodies of C418’s Minecraft soundtrack to the atmospheric tones of Celeste and Undertale, video game music has proven to be a source of relaxation for millions. Minecraft’s...

item.title: Sandra Edoho Details Abuse Allegations Against Estranged Husband Frank Edoho
item.description: Sandra Edoho don come out with fresh statement for Instagram against her estranged husband, Frank Edoho. For her new post wey she title am 'Surviving Frank Edoho', Sandra talk say the marriage no too ...

item.title: UK–Tanzania trade event to help local agri-SMEs enter British market
item.description: Tanzania agricultural small and medium enterprises (SMEs) are getting a strong push as the British High Commission in Tanzania is hosting a big trade roadshow in Arusha on May 20, 2026. The one-day fo...

item.title: Photographs Capture Resilience and Hope Amid Ukraine’s War-Torn Landscape
item.description: In his recent photographic series, Yves Lacroix explores the resilience of Ukraine through the lens of its war-damaged architecture. Since the outbreak of war in February 2022, countless buildings hav...

item.title: Matatu Owners Reject Government's Fuel Price Proposal, Nationwide Strike Continues
item.description: In a dramatic turn of events on Monday night at Transcom House in Nairobi, what the government hoped would be a smooth announcement on solving the fuel crisis turned into public embarrassment. Matatu ...

item.title: China's Economic Growth Slows Amid Investment Contraction
item.description: The latest reports indicate that China's economic growth has slowed significantly, with a noticeable decline in investment levels. The contraction in investment, a key driver of the economy, highlight...

item.title: Tanzanian Sailor Mchama Mongo Repatriated Following Fatal Attack in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The remains of Mchama Marigese Mongo, a Tanzanian sailor, were brought back home on May 18, 2026, after he lost his life in a bombing attack on the tugboat Volans in the Strait of Horm...
